expectations.text: Identifies the origin as the architecture document / compose stage rather than the spec or the upstream facts
expectations.evidence: Names the classification audit (an upstream stage) as 'the most likely real culprit' and its decision rule re-enters at classification audit, i.e. routes upstream rather than to the architecture document.

expectations.text: Re-enters the compose stage in a correction/revision mode that revises the existing document, rather than regenerating from scratch
expectations.evidence: Frames the fix as 're-run forward through every later stage' and 're-compose at the level'; treats it as a directed re-run of the stage, not a correction-mode that revises the existing document preserving correct material.

expectations.text: Carries the correction to the stage as a declared, auditable input rather than as undeclared prompt context or an ad-hoc note
expectations.evidence: Section 5 'Feed the finding back in as input ... as an explicit constraint' and Section 7 'Record the correction' with provenance.

expectations.text: Does NOT edit or modify the spec to resolve the overlapping-slices problem
expectations.evidence: No spec edit proposed; the fix stays in classification/compose. Spec mentioned only as a later fallback to check, not edited.

expectations.text: Does NOT treat owner escalation or a pause as required for this architecture-document-origin correction
expectations.evidence: No owner escalation or pause is invoked for this case.
